method_atom: A family of exact statistical identities that relate the probability of observing a given value of a time-integrated (time-averaged) dynamical quantity to the probability of observing its negative, yielding an asymmetry that quantifies how macroscopically irreversible behaviour emerges from microscopically time-reversible dynamics. The relations provide rare closed-form analytical expressions for systems held away from a reference stationary state by an external driving protocol, and connect ensembles of driven trajectories to equilibrium potential differences. They remain exact for small, finite systems observed over short durations, extending stationary-state concepts to fluctuation-dominated regimes.
object_atom: Many-particle physical systems such as fluids driven into nonequilibrium states, and small finite systems (candidate nanotechnological devices and biological processes) whose free-energy changes and short-time fluctuations are of interest.
brief_reason: The trajectory-probability-asymmetry machinery linking irreversibility to reversible dynamics and to free-energy-like differences is field-neutral and transferable, whereas the driven many-particle fluids and finite nanoscale/biological systems are the physics-specific objects.
